mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 4% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when the yield curve is inverted, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during liquidity crunches, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with t-statistic below 1.5 across 10-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 4.0 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of high-inflation periods or the signal is fully subsumed by federal funds rate changes in multivariate regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when wage growth exceeds 5% annually, or the joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing over 5-year horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue fundamentals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal shows zero predictive power with Rank IC equal to 0 over 5-year rolling windows or is fully priced in prior to quarterly data releases.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of declining industrial production indicate a contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered instances or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during manufacturing downturns.
